implemented: Added 6 structured (receiver, field) member-access entries to RUBY_AST_SOURCES (5: STDIN.read, STDIN.gets, STDIN.readline, File.read, File.open) and RUBY_AST_SINKS (1: IO.popen) in crates/tldr-core/src/security/taint.rs alongside the existing raw-substring ("", "X.y") duplicates. Updated the partial-coverage comment at L2365-2374 to note that Module.function shapes are now structurally matched via Ruby's call grammar (call / method_call node kinds with receiver + method children) and that M5 will atomically delete the raw-substring duplicates with the regex banks. Subscript entries (params[, ENV[) and call_names entries (gets / eval / system / exec / *.send) preserved verbatim. RUBY_PATTERNS regex bank untouched (M5 deletes). Sanitizer banks untouched. Per M1 architectural finding (member_patterns_match raw-substring fallback at taint.rs:3088-3092 catches Module.function shapes under analyze_ast_only via descendant_text.contains()), the structured entries are functionally additive in W2-pre — they will become load-bearing when M5 deletes both the regex banks AND the raw-substring duplicates atomically. No behavior change at the test level; M2 is preparatory.
harness_mechanism: N/A for M2 — uses the analyze_ast_only thread-local harness added in M1.

tests.rr_module_function_integ_test_summary: 21 total: 18 PASSED, 3 FAILED. The 6 Ruby Module.function fixtures on the regular path (analyze_with_ssa) ALL PASS: ruby_stdin_read_to_eval, ruby_stdin_gets_to_system, ruby_stdin_readline_to_io_popen, ruby_file_read_to_eval, ruby_file_open_to_system, ruby_io_popen_with_user_input (regular path). The 3 FAILED tests are identical to the M1 RED state (UNCHANGED): (a) ruby_io_popen_with_user_input AST-only path — bare `gets` parses as identifier not call_expression in Ruby tree-sitter; pre-existing bare-call AST coverage gap (M1 architectural_finding_2); (b) elixir_string_literal_system_cmd_substring_zero_findings REGULAR path — pre-existing string-literal regex/AST raw-fallback false positive (M1 architectural_finding_3); (c) ocaml_string_literal_sys_command_substring_zero_findings REGULAR path — pre-existing OCaml regex string-leak (M1 architectural_finding_3). All 3 FAILs are orthogonal to the M2 Module.function-shape rewrites and will resolve at M5 (when raw-fallback duplicates AND regex banks are deleted, structured-shape match correctly skips string contexts).
tests.rr_baseline_per_language_test_summary: 19 total: 19 PASSED including the 3 baseline Module.function tests (ruby_module_function_io_popen_baseline, elixir_module_function_system_cmd_baseline, ocaml_module_function_sys_command_baseline).
tests.rr_stdlib_integ_test_summary: All variants PASS — unchanged from M1.
tests.val_test_summary: val001a (PASS), val001b (PASS), val002_member_access_structural (5 PASS), val002_cfg_break_ssa_params (2 PASS), val002_whatbreaks_affected_tests (PASS), val003 (4 PASS) — all GREEN.
tests.lib_taint_tests_summary: All taint_tests::* lib tests PASS — unchanged from M1.
pre_existing_orthogonal_failures: ruby_io_popen_with_user_input_via_compute_taint AST-only path (M1 architectural_finding_2 — pre-existing bare-call gap)
tests.verification: Diffed M2 failing-test set against M1 RED state + W2-pre canonical orthogonal list. ZERO net new failures introduced by M2.

checks.exit_code: non-zero (pre-existing orthogonal failures only — see tests.pre_existing_orthogonal_failures; no M2-induced regressions)

files_per_change.purpose: Added 6 structured-shape (receiver, field) tuples to RUBY_AST_SOURCES (3 STDIN.* + 2 File.*) and RUBY_AST_SINKS (1 IO.popen) alongside existing raw-fallback duplicates; updated partial-coverage comment to reflect new structural matching path.
Full cargo test capture for audit trail.

loc_delta_actual.vs_contract_estimate: Contract estimated 18 LOC; actual 12 net (17 insertions, 5 deletions). Slight under-estimate because the existing entries already had multi-element member_patterns arrays — extending them in-place was cheaper than expected. No semantic difference vs contract.

discoveries.title: M1 architectural_finding_1 confirmed under M2: structured additions are functionally additive — no test transition observed
discoveries.details: M1 predicted that M2/M3/M4 structured rewrites would NOT be behavior-changing because member_patterns_match (taint.rs:3088-3092) has a raw-substring fallback that catches `("", "X.y")` entries via descendant_text.contains() under both regular and AST-only dispatch. Empirically confirmed at M2: the 21-test rr_module_function_integ_test breakdown is IDENTICAL to M1 (18 PASS / 3 FAIL, same 3 FAILs). The structured entries are now present and exercised on the structural path (verified by cargo check passing — match arms compile against the existing AstSourcePattern/AstSinkPattern shape) but provide no net new gating until M5 deletes the raw-fallback duplicates atomically.
discoveries.implication_for_m3_m4_m5: M3 (Elixir) and M4 (OCaml) should follow the same additive pattern — add structured entries alongside existing raw-fallback duplicates, expect zero test transitions, and confirm cargo check + no-new-regressions only. M5 ATOMIC remains the only milestone where structured entries become load-bearing.

discoveries.title: Existing entry shape allowed in-place array extension — no new AstSourcePattern / AstSinkPattern struct literals needed
discoveries.details: Initial reading of the M1 RUBY_AST_SOURCES suggested adding NEW AstSourcePattern struct entries for each of the 5 structured tuples. Closer inspection showed the existing entries (STDIN.* and File.*) already group all member_patterns of the same source_type into one AstSourcePattern with a multi-element member_patterns array. The M2 rewrite simply appends the structured tuples to the existing arrays — preserves struct count, minimizes diff, keeps source_type / sink_type assignment grouping logically clean. Same approach for RUBY_AST_SINKS IO.popen entry.
discoveries.implication: M3 / M4 workers should examine current entry grouping in ELIXIR_AST_* and OCAML_AST_* before deciding whether to extend in-place vs add new struct entries. Prefer in-place extension when the source_type / sink_type grouping permits.
